12.headline: Week 12 Recap: Coyotes Howl, Pooh Pounces, and Gamblino Keeps Cruising
12.intro: A week of statement wins and lopsided sheets, as four of five matchups ended in routs and only the contenders escaped unscathed.

matchups.narrative: David Glancy's Pirates flexed across the board, taking nine categories behind a tidy .302 average, .890 OPS, and a sparkling 1.088 WHIP. The Chicago Orphans countered with speed (5 SB), quality starts (3), and holds (2), but couldn't hang in the bulk hitting and ratio categories. Pittsburgh's rotation set the tone, allowing the Orphans little room to climb back.
matchups.day_by_day: The Orphans actually punched out a 22-strikeout opener on 06-15, but the Pirates seized control mid-week and never let go. A 7-run, 3-homer outburst on 06-20 effectively slammed the door before both teams limped through the final day.
matchups.standout_players: Bryan Reynolds powered the Pirates with a 2/5, 1 HR, 3 RBI line while Joey Cantillo dealt 8 innings of 9-K, 1.13 ERA ball. Bryce Harper kept Chicago respectable at 3/4 with a homer, and MacKenzie Gore struck out 10 over 7 frames in a losing cause.

matchups.narrative: Phoenix throttled the league's last-place squad, sweeping everything but runs and steals on the back of a monster pitching week (7 QS, 83 K, 3.51 ERA). All Betts Are Off managed to edge the R category 35-34 and dominate steals 7-1, but those were the only consolation prizes. The Coyotes' 17 homers and .894 OPS left no doubt.
matchups.day_by_day: Phoenix jumped ahead early with a 2-homer 06-15 and a 3-homer 06-16, building a cushion that All Betts never threatened. A 15-strikeout pitching day on 06-19 buried any comeback hopes before a quiet final weekend.
matchups.standout_players: Jac Caglianone smashed 2 homers with 3 RBI and 3 runs for the Coyotes, and Cam Schlittler punched out 13 over 6 scoreless. All Betts got strong efforts from William Contreras (4/5, HR, 3 RBI) and Paul Skenes (8 K, 3.00 ERA), but it wasn't nearly enough.

matchups.narrative: Kevin Glancy's crew put together a near-perfect week, winning 11 of 12 categories and surrendering only saves. The hitting led the way with 15 homers, 47 RBI, a .270 average, and a league-best 9 steals, while the staff posted nine quality starts and a glittering 2.96 ERA. Lets Go Bucs simply had no answer outside the bullpen, where their 6 saves at least avoided the shutout.
matchups.day_by_day: The Bucs actually opened strong on 06-15 with 6 runs and 3 homers, but Pooh's pitching surged with a 12-K, 13-K stretch and never relinquished the ratio categories. By the back half of the week the margins were academic.
matchups.standout_players: Bobby Witt Jr. went a perfect 2/2 with a homer and 2 RBI, and Nolan McLean tossed 7 scoreless with 9 strikeouts to anchor the rout. Dominic Canzone (1/3, HR, 2 RBI) was a rare bright spot for the Bucs.

matchups.narrative: The league's top seed took care of business, naturally, leading all teams with 20 homers and 53 RBI en route to a nine-category win. Of course, the Piglets did expose some cracks, grabbing steals, quality starts, and holds while Gamblino's 4.85 ERA and 1.365 WHIP looked decidedly ordinary for a first-place outfit. Still, the power and saves carried the day.
matchups.day_by_day: Pittsburgh Piglets actually outscored Gamblino 9-6 with 5 homers on opening day 06-15, a reminder this one wasn't a coronation. But a 5-homer barrage from the Gamblino bats on 06-20 settled matters before a sleepy finish.
matchups.standout_players: JJ Wetherholt led the way with 2 homers, 3 RBI and 3 runs, and Jesus Luzardo spun 7 innings of 9-K, 2.57 ERA work. Nick Kurtz answered for the Piglets with a homer and 3 RBI, but Logan Webb's 8 innings of 2.25 ball deserved a better supporting cast.

matchups.narrative: Cronen-Zone leaned on thunder and elite ratios, winning runs, homers, RBI, OPS, saves, and posting a week-best 2.678 ERA despite hitting just .210. Seattle countered with contact and strikeouts, taking AVG, SB, K, and QS while the two split holds. In the end the long ball and run prevention edged out the Dumpers' steadier profile.
matchups.day_by_day: Seattle stole 06-17 with a 7-run day, but Cronen-Zone's power surged late, capped by an enormous 9-run, 4-homer explosion on the final day 06-21 to clinch the win. That closing burst flipped a tight contest decisively.
matchups.standout_players: Pete Alonso (2/3, HR, 3 RBI) and TJ Rumfield (HR, 3 RBI) fueled the Cronen-Zone power, with Nathan Eovaldi and Shane Baz each fanning 9. Logan Gilbert was sharp for Seattle at 6.1 IP with 8 K and a 1.42 ERA, but the bats went quiet too often.

12.roto_section: The Big Gamblino paced the league in homers (20) and RBI (53), while the Pittsburgh Pirates topped runs (52), average (.302), and WHIP (1.088). Pooh's On First swept the speed-and-volume markers with 9 steals and 9 quality starts, and Cronen-Zone owned the run-prevention crown at a 2.678 ERA. Seattle's 95 strikeouts led all pitching staffs.
12.standings_note: The Big Gamblino (83-41-8) holds a commanding lead, but Phoenix (78-47-7) and a surging Pooh's On First (74-50-8) are keeping the chase honest. At the bottom, All Betts Are Off (26-99-7) continues to dig out of a deep hole.
